# crq Agent Guide

crq is the repo's CLI for autonomous CodeRabbit/Codex pull-request review loops.

Core rule: never post `@coderabbitai review` directly. Use `crq loop`.

Do not bypass crq to read review status, either. Never hand-poll the GitHub API
(`gh api .../pulls/N/reviews|comments`, looping on the head) to wait for a review
or its outcome: it drains the shared account-wide GitHub REST quota (also spent by
the `crq autoreview` daemon and every other agent, so it exhausts fast) and
competes with crq's own polling. To learn a review's result use `crq loop` (waits
and returns findings) or `crq feedback` (current findings, no trigger); for
queue/quota use `crq status`.

Readiness check:

```bash
crq doctor
```

`crq doctor` emits JSON for agents: crq config, `gh`, optional CodeRabbit CLI,
and `CODERABBIT_API_KEY` presence.

## Setup

The user must configure `~/.config/crq/env` once:

```bash
export CRQ_REPO=OWNER/crq-state
export CRQ_ISSUE=1
export CRQ_CAL_PR=1
export CRQ_SCOPE=OWNER
export CRQ_STATE_REF=crq-state
```

CodeRabbit native auto-review must be off.

## Agent Loop

```bash
crq loop OWNER/REPO PR_NUMBER > crq-feedback.json
rc=$?
```

`crq loop` queues the PR, triggers CodeRabbit when the account can spend a review, waits for every
configured required bot (`CRQ_REQUIRED_BOTS`) on the current head, then emits one JSON report.

Exit codes:
- `0`: done; converged or no actionable findings.
- `10`: read `.findings[]`, fix valid issues, validate, commit, push, resolve addressed threads, then repeat.
- `2`: timed out waiting for feedback; do not push a stale-feedback round.

Resolve addressed threads on GitHub (crq keys off GitHub resolution state; a finding keeps
reappearing until its thread is resolved there):

```bash
crq resolve OWNER/REPO PR_NUMBER --thread THREAD_ID
```

Decline a finding you are not addressing: post the reason on its thread (left unresolved; add
`--resolve` to also close it) instead of leaving it silently open:

```bash
crq decline OWNER/REPO PR_NUMBER --thread THREAD_ID --reason "why declined"
```

Current feedback without triggering a review:

```bash
crq feedback OWNER/REPO PR_NUMBER
```

`crq feedback`/`crq loop` also surface still-open findings from earlier commits (unresolved,
non-outdated review threads), so no manual cross-review audit is needed.

Long waits are not hangs. During queue/rate-limit waits, missing required-bot reviews, and
network/GitHub outages, crq logs progress to stderr; stdout stays reserved for the final JSON.

Agent communication rule: do not relay every stderr progress line or send repeated "still waiting"
updates. Tell the user once when a long `crq loop` wait begins, then stay quiet until the state
changes (fired, feedback wait, findings, convergence, timeout, rate-limit/window change, network
outage/recovery), the user asks for status, or at least 10 minutes have passed with no update.

Fleet review:

```bash
crq autoreview
```

Diagnostics only:

```bash
crq doctor
crq status
crq debug state
crq debug refresh
```

JSON contract: `findings` is always an array. Each finding always has `bot`,
`severity`, `title`, `body`, and `source`. The location fields `path`, `line`,
`url`, and `thread_id` are optional and omitted when CodeRabbit doesn't supply
them (e.g. review-body / outside-diff findings have no `path`/`line`, and only
inline review threads carry a `thread_id`) — parse them defensively.

Optional local preflight: if CodeRabbit CLI is installed, `crq preflight --type uncommitted`
can review local git changes before pushing and normalize the JSON stream. That does not replace
`crq loop`; only `crq loop` queues and waits for GitHub PR review feedback.
